title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight utility that allows calling React components as asynchronous functions. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow architectures to manage modals and similar components without React Portals, reducing state tracking and boilerplate code.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers react-call, a lightweight utility for invoking React components as asynchronous functions, presented as an alternative to React Portals for modals and similar UI. It matters because the usual approach to modals involves heavy state tracking and boilerplate to manage open state, results, and lifecycle. The key takeaways: react-call relies on Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow architectures to handle modal management, which cuts the state tracking and boilerplate that Portal-based patterns typically require.
